topStories.title: Brent crude oil price falls below $90 a barrel on hopes of Iran deal
topStories.summary: Oil prices fell around 5 percent to below $90 a barrel on Tuesday after US President Donald Trump again said an Iran peace deal could be close. The international benchmark Brent crude fell below $90 for the first time since April 14, while its US counterpart, West Texas Intermediate crude fell to around $86 a barrel.

topStories.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Wednesday
topStories.summary: Indian equities ended higher on Tuesday, supported by easing geopolitical tensions and softer crude prices. Analysts see potential for further recovery, though caution persists amid FII outflows, global uncertainties and weak near-term earnings outlook.

This is part of its broader PSU disinvestment strategy.
